title: Vibe code conversational agents in AI Studio
summary: Google for Developers video demonstrates how to build real-time conversational agents in Google AI Studio using natural language and voice input. The workflow includes describing an app, iterating on it, and publishing to a public URL.
writeup: Thor (Google DeepMind, Gemini API team) walks through building a real-time multilingual interview-practice agent inside Google AI Studio, using Gemini 2 Flash as the coding agent. The demo proceeds from voice-described spec to a working conversational app in under two minutes, then covers one-click publishing to Cloud Run and a branch-and-remix workflow for deriving a second product from the first. The through-line is that AI Studio collapses the full cycle, from idea to deployed URL to variant app, without leaving the browser.
key_points: Google AI Studio's coding agent, powered by Gemini 2 Flash, generates a complete real-time conversational application from a spoken natural-language description with no manual coding required.
The built app demonstrated live voice conversation that switches naturally between English and German mid-interview, fulfilling the multilingual capability spec stated in the original voice prompt.
Publishing deploys directly to Google Cloud Run, giving the app a dedicated public URL shareable outside the studio without any manual infrastructure setup.

The branch-and-remix feature lets a finished app serve as the starting point for a different product: the interview partner was remixed into a multilingual customer support agent that ingests uploaded PDF knowledge documents.
Google AI Studio's gallery includes multiplayer games, Google Maps integrations, and video generation examples built with VO, signaling the breadth of agentic app types the platform targets.
